title: angr Concolic Execution Pipeline
summary: A five-module chain in the concolic/symbolic execution domain built on the angr binary analysis framework. The first module establishes core binary analysis capabilities (CFG recovery, function discovery, symbolic path exploration). The second module implements Driller, which uses selective symbolic execution to augment fuzzing by generating inputs that cross compartment boundaries guarded by magic number checks. The third module implements Cozy, a comparative symbolic execution engine that analyzes two binary versions (pre-patch and post-patch) to identify behavioral differences and generate concrete witness inputs. The fourth module implements forward taint propagation to track user-controlled data flow through binaries and identify when tainted data reaches dangerous sinks. The fifth module implements symbolic vulnerability pattern detection combining taint analysis with constraint solving to detect buffer overflows, format strings, and use-after-free.

instruction:
You are implementing a concolic/symbolic execution pipeline using the angr binary
analysis framework. The workspace contains test binaries in test_binaries/ and empty
Python package directories. You must implement five modules in topological order:

1. angr_analysis/analyzer.py - Core binary analysis: CFG recovery, function discovery,
   symbolic path exploration
2. driller_lite/driller_main.py - Selective symbolic execution to generate inputs that
   cross compartment boundaries (magic number checks)
3. cozy_lite/analyzer.py - Comparative symbolic execution to compare pre/post-patch
   binaries and identify behavioral differences
4. taint_analysis/taint_tracker.py - Forward taint propagation engine that tracks
   user-controlled input through registers and memory, detecting when tainted data
   reaches dangerous sinks (memcpy, printf, system)
5. vuln_detector/scanner.py - Symbolic vulnerability pattern detection using constraint
   solving to identify buffer overflows, format string bugs, and use-after-free
   conditions, with concrete exploit input generation

Each module builds on angr (installed via pip). Update run.sh after each module to
output experiment results as KEY: VALUE lines. Requirements for each module are in
requirements/<slug>.yaml.

Test signatures:
- angr: analyze_binary(binary_path) -> dict with CFG_BASIC_BLOCKS, FUNCTIONS_DISCOVERED, SYMBOLIC_PATHS_CMD
- driller: DrillerLite(binary, input_bytes).drill() -> list of new input bytes
- cozy: CozyAnalysis(binary_pre, binary_post, input_size).run() -> dict with terminal states and diffs
- taint_analysis: TaintAnalysis(binary_path).analyze() -> dict with tainted_branches, taint_reach_ratio, sink_reaches
- vuln_detector: VulnScanner(binary_path).scan() -> list of VulnReport objects

moduleDag.description: A five-module chain in the concolic/symbolic execution domain built on the angr binary analysis framework. The first module establishes core binary analysis capabilities (CFG recovery, function discovery, symbolic path exploration). The second module implements Driller, which uses selective symbolic execution to augment fuzzing by generating inputs that cross compartment boundaries guarded by magic number checks. The third module implements Cozy, a comparative symbolic execution engine that analyzes two binary versions (pre-patch and post-patch) to identify behavioral differences and generate concrete witness inputs. The fourth module implements forward taint propagation to track user-controlled data flow through binaries and identify when tainted data reaches dangerous sinks. The fifth module implements symbolic vulnerability pattern detection combining taint analysis with constraint solving to detect buffer overflows, format strings, and use-after-free.

nodes.label: angr - Binary Analysis Platform
nodes.description: Core binary analysis module providing CFG recovery via CFGFast, function discovery, and symbolic path exploration using angr's simulation manager with symbolic stdin.

nodes.label: Driller - Selective Symbolic Execution
nodes.description: Implements the Driller algorithm: uses symbolic execution to explore all feasible paths through a binary, generating concrete inputs for each path. This enables crossing compartment boundaries (magic checks) that random fuzzing cannot easily overcome.

nodes.label: Cozy - Comparative Symbolic Execution
nodes.description: Comparative symbolic execution engine that runs two binary versions on shared symbolic inputs, compares terminal states for compatibility, identifies behavioral differences, and generates witness inputs.

nodes.label: Taint Analysis - Forward Taint Propagation
nodes.description: Forward dataflow taint tracking engine that marks user-controlled input (stdin, argv, environment) as taint sources and propagates taint through register assignments, memory loads/stores, and branch conditions. Detects when tainted data reaches dangerous sinks (memcpy, printf, system).

nodes.label: Vuln Detector - Symbolic Vulnerability Pattern Detection
nodes.description: Pattern-based vulnerability detector using symbolic constraint solving. Implements detectors for buffer overflows (write index exceeds bounds), format string bugs (symbolic format argument), and use-after-free (access to freed memory region). Generates concrete exploit inputs.

edges.label: Driller uses angr's symbolic execution engine
Cozy builds on angr for binary analysis and synthesis
Taint analysis uses angr for binary loading and symbolic execution
Vuln detector uses taint analysis to identify attacker-controlled data at sinks
Vuln detector leverages Driller's input generation for exploit synthesis
